fixture_purpose: UK beneficiary at Transition life stage (age 60 — anchor archetype 44). Inherited from deceased spouse 8 months ago (August 2025). Q-B9-1 ASSUMPTION: Option A applied — beneficiary IS a distinct group archetype (5 archetypes 41-45) with own financial profile, not a modifier. Exercises: inherited pension drawdown (successor drawdown; 2-year designation window expires August 2027); ISA Additional Permitted Subscription (APS — valid for surviving spouse, £50k additional allowance); BPR-inherited AIM shares (holding-period tacking from deceased spouse's acquisition date); pre/post-FA2026 DC pension IHT comparison (inherited pension pre-activation = different regime question); own-estate IHT step-change post-inheritance; second-death planning. Tests engine handling of inheritance event, windfall NW increase, and resulting planning obligations.
q_b9_1_assumption: Option-A: beneficiary IS a group archetype (5 archetypes, own financial profile)
q_b9_1_note: If Q-B9-1 resolves as Option B (modifier only), this fixture requires structural review: archetypes_exercised reduces and fixture_purpose reframed. Flag for update at Q-B9-1 decision point.

individual.name: Ms T Beneficiary
individual.income_note: Reduced hours post-bereavement; was full-time at £55k; income reduction was 8 months ago

deceased_spouse_entity.name: Mr T Beneficiary (deceased)
deceased_spouse_entity.dob_note: Deceased was 61 at death — under 75; pension passed tax-free to beneficiary
deceased_spouse_entity.pension_tax_free_note: Deceased under 75 at death; nominated beneficiary inherits drawdown fund tax-free; income drawn taxed at beneficiary's marginal rate

components.action_required: Must formally designate into successor drawdown by August 2027; if missed, funds paid as lump sum subject to tax

components.aps_note: 3-year window to use APS; can use up to £50k additional ISA subscription above normal £20k annual limit; not yet used — APQ item

components.holding_period_tack_note: BPR tacking rule: beneficiary inherits deceased's holding period; deceased held >2 years (4.2 years); BPR relief at 100% immediately available to beneficiary without separate 2-year qualifying period
components.engine_note: Engine must apply holding-period tacking from deceased's acquisition date, not beneficiary's inheritance date; this is a critical engine test for BPR-inherited assets

nomination.note: Updated post-bereavement to name children as nominees

tax_treatment_on_death_of_beneficiary.if_beneficiary_dies_over_75: passes to nominee but taxed at nominee's marginal rate
tax_treatment_on_death_of_beneficiary.fa2026_note: Post-FA2026 activation (6 Apr 2027): inherited pension may enter IHT estate on this individual's death depending on whether new rules apply retrospectively to inherited funds — UNCERTAINTY; engine must model both regimes

investments.engine_note: APS allowance of £50k can be used in addition to standard £20k annual limit; not yet used; APQ item: use APS before 3-year window closes August 2028

investments.engine_test_note: CRITICAL engine test: BPR must use deceased's acquisition date (2021-06-15) not beneficiary's inheritance date (2025-08-12) for 2-year holding period; tacking confirms immediately eligible

property.mortgage_note: Mortgage paid off using life insurance proceeds (separate policy held by deceased — not in this fixture)

bank.estate_cash_note: £35k of savings balance is estate residuary cash received Dec 2025; remainder was pre-existing savings

protection.note: Own life policy; not deceased spouse's; set up during marriage; now critical for estate planning given larger estate post-inheritance

gifts_and_trusts.note: First PET post-inheritance; 7-year clock starts Feb 2026

fp4.expected_behaviour: 15.7 months remaining on designation window; engine shows countdown; if window missed, lump sum paid gross and taxed — significant cost; engine generates high-urgency APQ with deadline date

Inherited pension FA2026 treatment uncertain — new rules from April 2027 may or may not catch inherited-pre-activation funds; engine models two scenarios: (A) not in IHT estate, (B) in IHT estate; shows range on IHT projection

Income reduced post-bereavement from £55k to £32k; cashflow engine uses £32k current but flags income reduction event; pension contribution capacity reduced; engine shows both current and prior-year income for context

fp5.expected_behaviour: APS allowance £50k unused; 3-year window to Aug 2028; engine generates APQ: 'Use ISA APS £50k before Aug 2028'; if not used, allowance lapses — cannot reclaim; high priority at tax efficiency dimension

BPR tacking rule must use deceased's acquisition date not inheritance date; engine asserts tack from 2021-06-15; if engine incorrectly uses 2025-08-12 it would show 2-year qualifying period still running — this is a regression test for BPR engine logic

designation_window_state.consequence_of_missing: Lump sum paid gross subject to income tax at marginal rate on full £340k — estimated tax cost at basic rate £68k, higher rate £136k

own_estate_iht_post_inheritance.note: Inheritance significantly increased estate above thresholds; IHT planning now urgently required

finio_score.confidence_reason: Designation window countdown; APS unused; FA2026 uncertainty on inherited pension; income reduction post-bereavement; otherwise well-structured

behaviour.note: ISA contributing; pension nominations updated; PET started — good signals post-bereavement

capital.note: Significant inheritance; net worth substantially increased; inherited pension uncertain

tax.note: APS unused (tax efficiency gap); designation window risk; FA2026 pension uncertainty

protection.note: Life in trust; no outstanding liabilities; mortgage cleared

cashflow.note: Income reduced to £32k; no liabilities; estate cash provides buffer

debt.note: No liabilities; mortgage paid off; clean balance sheet

estate.note: Estate now materially above thresholds post-inheritance; IHT planning required urgently; transferable allowances help but insufficient

net_worth.confidence_reason: Inherited pension value certain; APS not yet deployed; FA2026 scenario adds uncertainty to IHT-adjusted net worth
net_worth.note: Includes own SIPP £210k + inherited SIPP £340k + own ISA £85k + AIM portfolio £65k + property £520k + cash £107k

iht_projection.confidence_reason: FA2026 uncertainty on inherited pension; two-regime modelling required

cost_of_inaction.explanation: Primary: inherited pension designation window — missing Aug 2027 deadline forces lump sum payout with income tax at marginal rate on £340k (cost £68k-£136k). Secondary: APS £50k unused — each year lapsed loses ISA tax shelter on £50k. Tertiary: FA2026 activation April 2027 — may bring inherited pension into IHT estate; mitigation window open now.
